objective_gates_from_persisted_report.acceptance: persisted objective report accepted as terminal

discoverability.evidence: Section nav with five in-page anchors, skip link, ten headings, error-summary region are all discoverable. However there is no progressbar landmark and no explicit Save/Clear affordance, so persistence and progress state are less discoverable than the other candidates.

navigation.evidence: Anchor jumps for all five sections, skip link, Tab from top surfaces 58 focusable stops with zero hidden-ancestor leaks; error-summary link moves focus into the flagged field.

input_burden.evidence: Lean form with the same underlying fields; autosaves silently on input/change, so no explicit save tap is required. Slight negative is that the user cannot deliberately confirm 'progress is saved'.

error_prevention_recovery.evidence: Blank submit surfaces grouped error summary and moves focus; inline error on invalid postcode after blur, cleared when '2000' re-entered; missing review checkbox triggers scoped review error only.

feedback_status.evidence: Live status region announces safe-progress-saved and post-confirmation duplicate-block ('Synthetic order placed once. Confirmation ID SYN-2048.'). No progressbar landmark, so status coverage is a step behind the other candidates.

accessibility.evidence: Landmarks (header/main/nav/footer), heading outline, skip link, no focus stops on hidden ancestors in normal or compact mode, keyboard-completable end-to-end. Absence of progressbar is a discoverability point rather than an accessibility failure.

responsive_touch_ergonomics.evidence: 42 interactive controls measured at 390x844; every truly interactive control >=24px on both axes; layout renders cleanly at 320x568 and 360x800.

interruption_resumption.evidence: Safe reload restores contact/delivery/shipping with payment fields empty (autosave works). But there is no explicit Save button (user must trust silent autosave), and after successful placement a reload returns the form to editable state with Place order re-enabled — weaker duplicate-safety across a reload boundary.

latency_perception.evidence: Purely local, zero external requests (0/12 total); no perceptible submit latency; reduced-motion respected.

destructive_actions.evidence: Duplicate submit is a no-op with status message ('Synthetic order placed once. Confirmation ID SYN-2048.'). However there is NO user-initiated clear-progress affordance at all, so there is no Confirm/Cancel/Escape destructive-safety pattern to evaluate; recovery from a saved state requires understanding the underlying storage.

cognitive_load.evidence: Minimal chrome — the leanest of the three. Sections are labelled and ordered, plain-language help, no extra optional controls to scan or decide about.

visual_information_clarity.evidence: Totals block readable; synthetic-value help text distinguishes fictional data; confirmation region explicit. Lack of a progressbar reduces at-a-glance clarity of 'where am I in the flow'.

primary_qualitative_strengths: Leanest UI: fewest optional controls to reason about (lowest cognitive load).
Autosave on input/change removes the need for an explicit save tap.
Same clean error/inline recovery pattern as the other candidates.
primary_qualitative_frictions: No user-initiated Clear saved progress affordance — no Confirm/Cancel/Escape destructive-safety pattern present.
No explicit Save progress button; user must trust silent autosave.
No progressbar landmark; progress state is not surfaced explicitly.
Post-confirmation reload re-editable with Place order re-enabled — weaker duplicate-safety across a reload boundary than the strongest candidate.
